body_excerpt: {"credits": [], "cve_id": "CVE-2026-35368", "cvss": {"score": 7.8, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:L/AC:H/PR:L/UI:N/S:C/C:H/I:H/A:H"}, "cvss_severities": {"cvss_v3": {"score": 7.8, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:L/AC:H/PR:L/UI:N/S:C/C:H/I:H/A:H"}, "cvss_v4": {"score": 0.0, "vector_string": null}}, "cwes": [{"cwe_id": "CWE-426", "name": "Untrusted Search Path"}], "description": "A vulnerability exists in the chroot utility of uutils coreutils when using the --userspec option. The utility resolves the user specification via getpwnam() after entering the chroot but before dropping root privileges. On glibc-based systems, this can trigger the Name Service Switch (NSS) to load shared libraries (e.g., libnss_*.so.2) from the new root directory. If the NEWROOT is writable by an attacker, they can inject a malicious NSS module to execute arbitrary code as root, facilitating a full container escape or privilege escalation.", "epss": {"percentage": 0.00136, "percentile": 0.03371}, "ghsa_id": "GHSA-mh5c-xrmh-m794", "github_reviewed_at": "2026-04-30T17:50:13Z", "html_url": "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-mh5c-xrmh-m794", "identifiers": [{"type": "GHSA", "value": "GHSA-mh5c-xrmh-m794"}, {"type": "CVE", "value": "CVE-2026-35368"}], "nvd_published_at": "2026-04-22T17:16:40Z", "published_at": "2026-04-22T18:31:45Z", "references": ["https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-35368", "https://github.com/uutils/coreutils/issues/10327", "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-mh5c-xrmh-m794"], "repository_advisory_url": null, "severity": "high", "source_code_location": "https://github.com/uutils/coreutils", "summary": "uutils coreutils has an Untrusted Search Path", "type": "reviewed", "updated_at": "2026-04-30T17:50:13Z", "url": "https://api.github.com/advisories/GHSA-mh5c-xrmh-m794", "vulnerabilities": [{"first_patched_version": null, "package": {"ecosystem": "rust", "name": "coreutils"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": "<= 0.8.0"}], "withdrawn_at": null}
